candidates.notes: Absorption is extremely rare (0.19% vs predicted >20%). Metric may be too strict or phenomenon manifests differently. 8 perfect-score latents are suspicious (exactly 100 activations each).

SAE bottleneck reduces faithfulness vs raw residual, but the key test (low-absorption vs high-absorption SAE latents) shows no difference. Both subsets yield 0.0 faithfulness, suggesting the latent selection method is flawed rather than absorption driving the effect.

h1_pilot.key_result: Only 0.19% of latents show absorption score >0.5, 100x below hypothesis threshold

h3_pilot.key_result: No monotonic relationship between L0 sparsity and absorption rate. Spearman r=0.086 (p=0.872). Absorption peaks at mid-layers (layer 4: 49.3%) rather than increasing with sparsity.

h4_pilot.key_result: SAE patching faithfulness (0.289) is below raw residual (0.400), but low-absorption (0.0) and high-absorption (0.0) latents both fail to restore signal. No meaningful difference between low/high absorption subsets.
